heading: Definitions
label: 2681 Definitions

children.label: A
children.text: the removal of lead-based paint and lead-contaminated dust, the permanent containment or encapsulation of lead-based paint, the replacement of lead-painted surfaces or fixtures, and the removal or covering of lead-contaminated soil; and

children.label: B
children.text: all preparation, cleanup, disposal, and postabatement clearance testing activities associated with such measures.

children.heading: Accessible surface
children.label: 2 Accessible surface
children.text: The term “accessible surface” means an interior or exterior surface painted with lead-based paint that is accessible for a young child to mouth or chew.

children.heading: Deteriorated paint
children.label: 3 Deteriorated paint
children.text: The term “deteriorated paint” means any interior or exterior paint that is peeling, chipping, chalking or cracking or any paint located on an interior or exterior surface or fixture that is damaged or deteriorated.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: 4 Evaluation
children.text: The term “evaluation” means risk assessment, inspection, or risk assessment and inspection.

children.heading: Friction surface
children.label: 5 Friction surface
children.text: The term “friction surface” means an interior or exterior surface that is subject to abrasion or friction, including certain window, floor, and stair surfaces.

children.heading: Impact surface
children.label: 6 Impact surface
children.text: The term “impact surface” means an interior or exterior surface that is subject to damage by repeated impacts, for example, certain parts of door frames.

children.heading: Inspection
children.label: 7 Inspection
children.text: The term “inspection” means (A) a surface-by-surface investigation to determine the presence of lead-based paint, as provided in , and (B) the provision of a report explaining the results of the investigation.section 4822(c) of title 42

children.heading: Interim controls
children.label: 8 Interim controls
children.text: The term “interim controls” means a set of measures designed to reduce temporarily human exposure or likely exposure to lead-based paint hazards, including specialized cleaning, repairs, maintenance, painting, temporary containment, ongoing monitoring of lead-based paint hazards or potential hazards, and the establishment and operation of management and resident education programs.

children.heading: Lead-based paint
children.label: 9 Lead-based paint
children.text: The term “lead-based paint” means paint or other surface coatings that contain lead in excess of 1.0 milligrams per centimeter squared or 0.5 percent by weight or (A) in the case of paint or other surface coatings on target housing, such lower level as may be established by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, as defined in , or (B) in the case of any other paint or surface coatings, such other level as may be established by the Administrator.section 4822(c) of title 42

children.heading: Lead-based paint hazard
children.label: 10 Lead-based paint hazard
children.text: The term “lead-based paint hazard” means any condition that causes exposure to lead from lead-contaminated dust, lead-contaminated soil, lead-contaminated paint that is deteriorated or present in accessible surfaces, friction surfaces, or impact surfaces that would result in adverse human health effects as established by the Administrator under this subchapter.

children.heading: Lead-contaminated dust
children.label: 11 Lead-contaminated dust
children.text: The term “lead-contaminated dust” means surface dust in residential dwellings that contains an area or mass concentration of lead in excess of levels determined by the Administrator under this subchapter to pose a threat of adverse health effects in pregnant women or young children.

children.heading: Lead-contaminated soil
children.label: 12 Lead-contaminated soil
children.text: The term “lead-contaminated soil” means bare soil on residential real property that contains lead at or in excess of the levels determined to be hazardous to human health by the Administrator under this subchapter.

children.heading: Reduction
children.label: 13 Reduction
children.text: The term “reduction” means measures designed to reduce or eliminate human exposure to lead-based paint hazards through methods including interim controls and abatement.

children.label: A
children.text: a single-family dwelling, including attached structures such as porches and stoops; or

children.label: B
children.text: a single-family dwelling unit in a structure that contains more than 1 separate residential dwelling unit, and in which each such unit is used or occupied, or intended to be used or occupied, in whole or in part, as the home or residence of 1 or more persons.

children.heading: Residential real property
children.label: 15 Residential real property
children.text: The term “residential real property” means real property on which there is situated 1 or more residential dwellings used or occupied, or intended to be used or occupied, in whole or in part, as the home or residence of 1 or more persons.

children.label: A
children.text: information gathering regarding the age and history of the housing and occupancy by children under age 6;

children.label: B
children.text: visual inspection;

children.label: C
children.text: limited wipe sampling or other environmental sampling techniques;

children.label: D
children.text: other activity as may be appropriate; and

children.label: E
children.text: provision of a report explaining the results of the investigation.

children.heading: Target housing
children.label: 17 Target housing
children.text: The term “target housing” means any housing constructed prior to 1978, except housing for the elderly or persons with disabilities or any 0-bedroom dwelling (unless any child who is less than 6 years of age resides or is expected to reside in such housing). In the case of jurisdictions which banned the sale or use of lead-based paint prior to 1978, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, at the Secretary’s discretion, may designate an earlier date.
